response.justification: The candidate has roughly 19 years of hands-on software development experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, which satisfies the tenure minimum. However, the role is a director-level individual contributor focused on AI strategy, and the resume demonstrates neither recognized AI expertise nor a track record of shipped AI products or platforms — the AI exposure is limited to recent MCP tooling contributions. There is also no evidence of the strategic advisory, organizational influence, or engineering leadership scope this position requires; the trajectory is consistently senior/staff-level product engineering.
strengths: ~19 years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Founder experience building and operating an open-source geo-data platform end to end, including Kubernetes deployment
Strong engineering craft signals: TDD, CI/CD, open-source libraries, workshops, and conference talks
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML products, platforms, or recognized AI expertise beyond recent MCP/agent tooling contributions
No director/principal-level scope: no evidence of setting technical strategy, advising executive customers, or leading across teams
Cloud experience is application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) rather than cloud platform strategy or large-scale infrastructure design

key_factors.factor: AI expertise gap vs. AI-strategy mandate
Long, versatile engineering track record with entrepreneurial founder experience
